entities.name: P11 - Failure Modes and Recovery
entities.context: Architectural principle for handling failures gracefully through pausing on uncertainty and logging incidents

entities.name: quality
entities.context: Category this principle belongs to in V's principle taxonomy

entities.name: uncertainty threshold
entities.context: 25% threshold above which the system should pause for better input

entities.name: incident logging
entities.context: Technique of writing minimal incident notes to logs before stopping on exceptions

entities.name: destructive operations
entities.context: Operations that could corrupt data or cause irreversible changes, requiring special handling

relationships.context: Principle is categorized under quality in V's principle system
Principle prescribes logging incidents with context before halting
Principle uses 25% uncertainty threshold as decision point for pausing
Principle specifically addresses halting before destructive actions on exceptions
